authors: Goggins WB; Woo J; Ho S; Chan EYY; Chau PH
title: Weather season and daily stroke admissions in Hong Kong
outcome: Hemorrhagic and ischemic stroke admissions separately
cold_definition: IS association below 22C; HS linear across range
lag_structure: HS lags 0-4; IS lags 0-13 below threshold
main_result: HS: 2.7% higher per 1C lower (lags 0-4); IS: 1.6% higher per 1C lower below 22C (lags 0-13)
limitations: Daily resolution; earlier period; pollutants NS
relevance_to_current_project: Historical stroke baseline; supports separate IS/HS models
notes: DOI to confirm from publisher; abstract verified

authors: Guo YT; Chan KH; Qiu H; Wong ELY; Ho KF
title: The risk of hospitalization associated with hot nights and excess nighttime heat in a subtropical metropolis: a time-series study in Hong Kong 2000-2019
temperature_metric: Hot-night metrics including HNday28 and hot-night excess
heat_definition: Official hot night Tmin>=28 and intensity metrics
sex_analysis: Hot-night excess associated with hospitalization independent of mean temperature; official HNday28 alone not associated with excess risk; circulatory admissions sensitive

limitations: HIGH overlap on hot nights; novelty must be narrowed
source_verified: Must cite; official binary hot night may be weak vs intensity
